title: Session Store Redis Desynchronization Causing Random Logouts
symptoms: Users reporting being randomly logged out every few minutes
Session lookup success rate at approximately 50% (should be 100%)
Redis cluster showing replication offset mismatch between nodes
Application logs showing alternating session-found and session-not-found for same user
Health check showing both Redis nodes as healthy individually
root_cause: Redis replication failure caused session data split between two nodes with inconsistent state, leading to random session lookup failures depending on which node served the request
resolution: Forced full resync of Redis replica from primary, restarted the session proxy to clear routing inconsistencies, and implemented session data verification on lookup
preventive_actions: Implement Redis replication monitoring with offset difference alerting
Add session lookup consistency check with automatic fallback to primary
Deploy Redis Sentinel for automatic failover and replication health
Implement session backup in application-side encrypted cookies as fallback
Add session store health endpoint checking replication consistency

timeline.event: Redis replication failure occurs, nodes become desynchronized
Support tickets accumulate, pattern of intermittent access identified
James Wright investigates session store, discovers replication offset mismatch
Redis replication desync confirmed as root cause
Full resync initiated from primary to replica
Session proxy restarted to clear routing inconsistencies
Session lookups returning to 100% success rate, incident resolved

embedding_summary: Redis session store desynchronization from failed replication event caused random logouts for 50% of user sessions. 8,400 users affected for 35 minutes with intermittent authentication failures. Resolved by forcing full replication resync and implementing replication health monitoring.
